Knockinaam: heaven’s door

It was love at first sight for me. As I topped the brow of a hill, laid out below was a perfectly proportioned country lodge facing down a vast green lawn to a rocky beach. Beyond it twinkled the Irish Sea. The author John Buchan fell for Knockinaam Lodge too, so enamoured on his first glimpse that he included it in The Thirty-Nine Steps, his 1915 spy novel.It was love at first sight for me.
